Identification.LongTitle: An Act for granting to His Majesty certain sums of money for the federal public administration for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026

Provision.Text: This enactment grants the sum of $149,771,149,015 towards defraying charges and expenses of the federal public administration for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026 that are not otherwise provided for.
MOST GRACIOUS SOVEREIGN,

Whereas it appears by message from Her Excellency the Right Honourable Mary Simon, Governor General and Commander-in-Chief of Canada, and the Estimates accompanying that message, that the sums mentioned below are required to defray certain expenses of the federal public administration, not otherwise provided for, for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026, and for other purposes connected with the federal public administration;

May it therefore please Your Majesty, that it may be enacted, and be it enacted by the King’s Most Excellent Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, that:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Appropriation Act No. 1, 2025–26.

There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund a sum not exceeding in the aggregate $149,771,149,015 towards defraying the various charges and expenses of the federal public administration for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026, not otherwise provided for, which is the total of the following amounts:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $144,228,741,594, which is the total of the amounts of the items set out in column 5 of Schedule 1 to this Act and corresponds to the total of the amounts of the items set out in the Proposed Schedule 1 in the Annex to the Main Estimates for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026 that were not appropriated by the special warrant issued under Order in Council P.C. 2025-462 of April 1, 2025 or the special warrant issued under Order in Council P.C. 2025-467 of May 2, 2025; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $5,542,407,421, which is the total of the amounts of the items set out in column 5 of Schedule 2 to this Act and corresponds to the total of the amounts of the items set out in the Proposed Schedule 2 in the Annex to the Main Estimates for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026 that were not appropriated by those special warrants.

Subsection.Text: The amount authorized by this Act to be paid in respect of an item may be paid only for the purposes, and subject to any terms and conditions, specified in the item.
The provisions of each item set out in Schedules 1 and 2 are deemed to have effect as of April 1, 2025.

Section.MarginalNote: Adjustments in accounts of Canada — Schedule 1
Section.Text: An appropriation that is granted by this Act or any other Act and referred to in Schedule 1 may be charged after the end of the fiscal year for which the appropriation is granted at any time before the day on which the Public Accounts for that fiscal year are tabled in Parliament, for the purpose of making adjustments in the accounts of Canada for that fiscal year that do not require payments out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

Section.MarginalNote: Adjustments in accounts of Canada — Schedule 2

Subsection.Text: An appropriation that is granted by this Act or any other Act and referred to in Schedule 2 may be charged after the end of the fiscal year following the fiscal year for which the appropriation is granted at any time before the day on which the Public Accounts for that second fiscal year are tabled in Parliament, for the purpose of making adjustments in the accounts of Canada for that second fiscal year that do not require payments out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

Despite any other provision of this Act, amounts appropriated by this Act and set out in Schedule 2 may be paid and applied at any time on or before March 31, 2027, so long as every payment is charged first against the relevant amount appropriated under any Act that is earliest in time until that amount is exhausted, next against the relevant amount appropriated under any other Act, including this Act, that is next in time until that amount is exhausted and so on. The balance of amounts so appropriated by this Act that have not been charged, subject to the adjustments referred to in section 37 of the Financial Administration Act, lapse at the end of the fiscal year following the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026.

Provision.Text: Based on the Main Estimates 2025–26, the amount granted is $144,228,741,594, which is the total of the amounts of the items set out in column 5 of this Schedule.
Sums granted to His Majesty by this Act for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026 and the purposes for which they are granted.

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: ADMINISTRATIVE TRIBUNALS SUPPORT SERVICE OF CANADA
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Service canadien d’appui aux tribunaux administratifs

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures
– Authority to make recoverable expenditures in relation to the application of the Canada Pension Plan and the Employment Insurance Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence de promotion économique du Canada atlantique

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Énergie atomique du Canada limitée

Provision.Text: – Payments to the corporation for operating and capital expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Conseil des Arts du Canada

Provision.Text: – Payments to the Council to be used for the furtherance of the objects set out in section 8 of the Canada Council for the Arts Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Société canadienne d’hypothèques et de logement

Provision.Text: – Payments to reimburse the Corporation for the amounts of loans forgiven, grants, contributions and expenditures made, and losses, costs and expenses incurred,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: under the National Housing Act; or

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: in the course of the exercise of powers or the carrying out of duties or functions conferred on the Corporation under any other Act of Parliament, in accordance with the Corporation’s authority under the Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation Act

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Centre de la sécurité des télécommunications

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from its operations, including the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Commission du droit d’auteur

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Service correctionnel du Canada

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions
– Authority to deposit into the Inmate Welfare Fund revenue derived during the fiscal year from projects operated by inmates and financed by that Fund
– Authority to operate canteens in federal institutions and to deposit, during the fiscal year, revenue from sales into the Inmate Welfare Fund
– Payments, in accordance with terms and conditions prescribed by the Governor in Council,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: to or on behalf of discharged inmates who suffer physical disability caused by participation in normal program activity in federal institutions; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: to dependants of deceased inmates and discharged inmates whose deaths resulted from participation in normal program activity in federal institutions
– Authority for the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness, subject to the approval of the Governor in Council, to enter into an agreement with any province for

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the confinement in institutions of that province of any persons sentenced, committed or transferred to a penitentiary;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: compensation for the maintenance of such persons; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: payment in respect of the construction and related costs of such institutions

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act
– Capital expenditures, including

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: payments to Indigenous governing bodies or Indigenous organizations, as defined in section 79 of the Corrections and Conditional Release Act, in connection with the provision of correctional services under an agreement referred to in section 81 of that Act; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: payments to non-profit organizations involved in community corrections operations, provinces and municipalities towards their respective construction costs

– To reduce from $11,000,000 to $5,000,000 the amount by which the aggregate of expenditures made for the purposes of the CORCAN Revolving Fund, established by Correctional Service Vote 11c, Appropriation Act No. 4, 1991–92, may exceed that Fund’s revenues

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Service administratif des tribunaux judiciaires

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère des Femmes et de l’Égalité des genres

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère de l’Agriculture et de l’Agroalimentaire

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from

Provision.Text: collaborative research agreements and research services;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: the administration of the AgriStability program; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– Capital expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère du Patrimoine canadien

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the activities of the Canadian Conservation Institute, the Canadian Heritage Information Network and the Canadian Audio-Visual Certification Office;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: activities undertaken under the Capital Experience Program; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère de la Citoyenneté et de l’Immigration

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year from the provision of services related to International Experience Canada — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from the provision of those services

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– Capital expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions, including the provision of goods and services

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: DEPARTMENT OF CROWN-INDIGENOUS RELATIONS AND NORTHERN AFFAIRS
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère des Relations Couronne-Autochtones et des Affaires du Nord

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures
– Expenditures on works, buildings and equipment
– Authority to make expenditures — recoverable or otherwise — on work performed on property that is not federal property and on services provided in respect of that property
– Authority to provide, in respect of Indian and Inuit economic development activities, for the capacity development for Indians and Inuit and the furnishing of materials and equipment

– Authority to sell electric power to private consumers in remote locations when alternative local sources of supply are not available, in accordance with terms and conditions approved by the Governor in Council

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset related expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– Capital expenditures
– Expenditures on buildings, works, land and equipment the operation, control and ownership of which

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: may be transferred to provincial governments on terms and conditions approved by the Governor in Council; or

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: may be transferred to Indian bands, groups of Indians or individual Indians at the discretion of the Minister of Crown-Indigenous Relations
– Expenditures on buildings, works, land and equipment that are on other than federal property
– Authority to make recoverable expenditures on roads and related works in amounts not exceeding the shares of provincial governments of expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

– Loans to Indigenous claimants in accordance with terms and conditions approved by the Governor in Council for the purpose of defraying costs related to research, development and negotiation of claims

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: DEPARTMENT OF EMPLOYMENT AND SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère de l’Emploi et du Développement social

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures
– Authority to make recoverable expenditures in relation to the application of the Canada Pension Plan and the Employment Insurance Act

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset related expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the provision of services to assist provinces in the administration of provincial programs funded under Labour Market Development Agreements;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: any amount charged to a Crown corporation under paragraph 14(b) of the Government Employees Compensation Act in relation to the litigation costs for subrogated claims for Crown corporations; and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: the portion of the Government Employees Compensation Act departmental or agency subrogated claim settlements related to litigation costs

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

– The writing off, as referred to in subsection 25(2) of the Financial Administration Act, of 19,291 debts due to His Majesty in right of Canada amounting to $197,249,543 related to student loans and apprentice loans made under the Canada Student Financial Assistance Act and the Apprentice Loans Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère des Finances

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset related expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State

– Under subsection 8(2) of the Bretton Woods and Related Agreements Act, the amount of financial assistance provided by the Minister of Finance by way of direct payments to the International Development Association is not to exceed $486,916,000 in Canadian dollars in the fiscal year 2025–26

– Under subsection 8(2) of the Bretton Woods and Related Agreements Act, the amount of financial assistance provided by the Minister of Finance by way of grants to the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development for the purpose of the Extraordinary Revenue Acceleration is not to exceed $200,000,000 in the fiscal year 2025–26

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère des Pêches et des Océans

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures
– Canada’s share of expenses of the international fisheries commissions
– Authority to provide free office accommodation for the international fisheries commissions
– Authority to make recoverable advances in the amounts of the shares of the international fisheries commissions of joint cost projects

– Authority to make recoverable advances for transportation, stevedoring and other shipping services performed for individuals, outside agencies and other governments in the course of, or arising out of, the exercise of jurisdiction in navigation, including aids to navigation and shipping

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: in the course of, or arising from, the activities of the Canadian Coast Guard; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: from the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– Capital expenditures
– Authority to make payments to provinces, municipalities and local or private authorities as contributions towards construction done by those bodies
– Authority for the purchase and disposal of commercial fishing vessels
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: DEPARTMENT OF FOREIGN AFFAIRS, TRADE AND DEVELOPMENT
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère des Affaires étrangères, du Commerce et du Développement

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures, including those related to the appointment of Canada’s representatives abroad, to the staff of those representatives, and to the assignment of Canadians to the staffs of international organizations
– Authority to make recoverable advances to international organizations in amounts not exceeding the amounts of the shares of those organizations
– Expenditures in respect of the provision of office accommodation for the International Civil Aviation Organization
– Authority to make recoverable expenditures for assistance to and repatriation of distressed Canadian citizens and Canadian residents who are abroad, including their dependants

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset related expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: trade and education fairs;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: departmental publications; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: the following services:

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: training services provided by the Canadian Foreign Service Institute,

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: trade missions and other international business development services,

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: investment development services,

Provision.Label: (iv)
Provision.Text: international telecommunication services,

Provision.Label: (v)
Provision.Text: other services provided abroad to other departments and to agencies, Crown corporations and non-federal organizations, and

Provision.Label: (vi)
Provision.Text: specialized consular services

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– Capital expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions, including payments for other specified purposes and for the provision of goods and services for

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the promotion of trade and investment; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: international humanitarian assistance and assistance in relation to international security, international development and global peace
– Payments made

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: in respect of pension, insurance and social security programs or other arrangements for employees locally engaged outside of Canada; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: in respect of the administration of such programs or arrangements, including premiums, contributions, benefit payments, fees and other expenditures made in respect of such employees and for any other persons that the Treasury Board determines

– Under subsection 12(2) of the International Development (Financial Institutions) Assistance Act, the amount of financial assistance provided by the Minister of Foreign Affairs, in consultation with the Minister of Finance, by way of direct payments for the purpose of contributions to the international financial institutions may not exceed $250,404,568 in the fiscal year 2025–26
– Loans under paragraph 3(1)(a) of the International Financial Assistance Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère de la Santé

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the provision of services or the sale of products related to health, to well-being and to regulatory activities; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

– Authority to enter into commitments in the fiscal year for the purposes of this Vote up to an amount that does not, at any time during the fiscal year, exceed the total unencumbered balance available out of

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: any appropriation by Parliament for the fiscal year for Vote 1, 5 or 10 of the Department,

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: any item included in estimates before the House of Commons for the fiscal year for Vote 1, 5 or 10 of the Department, and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: any revenues referred to in paragraph 32(1)(d) of the Financial Administration Act of the Department

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– Capital expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions, in the form of monetary payments or the provision of goods or services

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING, INFRASTRUCTURE AND COMMUNITIES
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère du Logement, de l’Infrastructure et des Collectivités

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– Capital expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère des Services aux Autochtones

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures
– Expenditures on works, buildings and equipment
– Authority to make expenditures — recoverable or otherwise — on work performed on property that is not federal property and on services provided in respect of that property
– Authority to provide, in respect of Indian and Inuit economic development activities, for the capacity development for Indians and Inuit and the furnishing of materials and equipment

– Authority to sell electric power to private consumers in remote locations when alternative local sources of supply are not available, in accordance with terms and conditions approved by the Governor in Council

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset related expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the provision of services or the sale of products related to health protection and medical services; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– Capital expenditures
– Expenditures on buildings, works, land and equipment the operation, control and ownership of which

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: may be transferred to provincial governments on terms and conditions approved by the Governor in Council; or

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: may be transferred to Indian bands, groups of Indians or individual Indians at the discretion of the Minister of Indigenous Services
– Expenditures on buildings, works, land and equipment that are on other than federal property
– Authority to make recoverable expenditures on roads and related works in amounts not exceeding the shares of provincial governments of expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions, in the form of monetary payments or the provision of goods or services

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère de l’Industrie

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act, and the provision of internal support services to the Canadian Intellectual Property Office;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: activities and operations related to communications research at the Communications Research Centre;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: services and insolvency processes under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act at the Office of the Superintendent of Bankruptcy;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: activities and operations carried out by Corporations Canada under the Canada Business Corporations Act, the Boards of Trade Act, the Canada Cooperatives Act and the Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act; and

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: services and regulatory processes for mergers and merger-related matters, including pre-merger notifications, advance ruling certificates and written opinions, under the Competition Act at the Competition Bureau

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– Capital expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions
– Payments under subsection 14(2) of the Department of Industry Act
– Loans under paragraph 14(1)(a) of the Department of Industry Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère de la Justice

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the provision of mandatory legal services to federal departments and agencies;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: the provision to Crown corporations, non-federal organizations and international organizations of optional legal services that are consistent with the Department’s mandate; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère de la Défense nationale

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– Authority for total commitments, subject to allotment by the Treasury Board, of $86,760,127,023 for the purposes of Votes 1, 5 and 10 of the Department regardless of the year in which the payment of those commitments comes due (of which it is estimated that $52,954,717,761 will come due for payment in future years)

– Authority, subject to the direction of the Treasury Board, to make recoverable expenditures or advances in respect of materials supplied to, or services performed on behalf of, individuals, corporations, outside agencies, other federal departments and agencies and other governments
– Payments

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: in respect of pension, insurance and social security programs or other arrangements for employees locally engaged outside of Canada; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: in respect of the administration of such programs or arrangements, including premiums, contributions, benefit payments, fees and other expenditures made in respect of such employees and for any other persons that the Treasury Board determines

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset related expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year, including from the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– Capital expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year and contributions, which grants and contributions may include

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: monetary payments or, in lieu of payment made to a recipient,

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: the provision of goods or services, or

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: the provision of the use of facilities; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: the contributions that may be approved by the Governor in Council in accordance with section 3 of The Defence Appropriation Act, 1950

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: for the provision or transfer of defence equipment,

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: for the provision of services for defence purposes, or

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: for the provision or transfer of supplies or facilities for defence purposes
– Payments

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: in respect of insurance and benefit programs or other arrangements for members of the Regular Force and the Reserve Force of the Canadian Forces; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: in respect of the administration of such programs or arrangements, including premiums, contributions, benefit payments, fees and other expenditures made in respect of such members and for any other persons that the Treasury Board determines

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère des Ressources naturelles

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the sale of forestry and information products;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: the issue of licences, permits and certificates under the Explosives Act and the Explosives Regulations, 2013;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: training and certification activities related to the Act and Regulations referred to in paragraph (b);

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: research, consultation, testing, analysis and administration services and research products as part of the departmental operations; and

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of the Financial Administration Act

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– Capital expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC SAFETY AND EMERGENCY PREPAREDNESS
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère de la Sécurité publique et de la Protection civile

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures
– Capital expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset related expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year including from the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC WORKS AND GOVERNMENT SERVICES
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère des Travaux publics et des Services gouvernementaux

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures for the provision of accommodation, common and central services
– Authority to make recoverable expenditures in relation to the application of the Canada Pension Plan, the Employment Insurance Act and the Seized Property Management Act
– Authority to expend revenues that it receives during the fiscal year arising from the provision of accommodation, common and central services

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year including from the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– Capital expenditures including expenditures on works other than federal property
– Authority to reimburse lessees of federal real property or federal immovables for improvements authorized by the Minister of Public Works and Government Services

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère de l’Environnement

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures
– Authority for the Minister of the Environment to engage consultants as required by different boards at the remuneration that those boards determine

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset related expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act and from the provision of services, the sale of information products, the collection of entrance fees, the granting of leases or the issuance of licenses or other authorizations, including

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: research, analysis and scientific services;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: hydrometric surveys;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: regulatory services;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: monitoring services, including monitoring services with respect to the oil sands;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: fees for entry to federal real property or federal immovables;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: permits; and

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: services in respect of federal real property or federal immovables, including the granting of surface leases to oil and gas companies and the issuance of licences for grazing

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– Capital expenditures
– Authority to make payments to provinces and municipalities as contributions towards construction done by those bodies
– Authority to make recoverable advances not exceeding the amount of the shares of provincial and outside agencies of the cost of joint projects including expenditures on other than federal property
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year

– Contributions, including ones to developing countries by way of the Multilateral Fund for the Implementation of the Montreal Protocol, taking the form of monetary payments or the provision of goods, equipment or services

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère des Transports

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures
– Authority to make expenditures on other than federal property in the course of, or arising out of the exercise of jurisdiction in, aeronautics
– Authority for the payment of commissions for revenue collection under the Aeronautics Act

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year including from the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– Capital expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère des Anciens Combattants

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– Expenditures related to the upkeep of real property or immovable property, including engineering and other investigatory planning expenses that do not add tangible value to the property, payment of taxes, insurance and public utilities
– Expenditures related to, subject to the approval of the Governor in Council,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: necessary remedial work on properties constructed under individual firm price contracts and sold under the Veterans’ Land Act (R.S.C. 1970, c. V-4), to correct defects for which neither the veteran nor the contractor may be held financially responsible; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: other work on other properties that is required to protect the Director’s interest or right in those properties

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year; however, the amount listed for any grant may be increased or decreased, subject to the approval of the Treasury Board
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère de la Diversification de l’économie de l’Ouest canadien

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset related expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT AGENCY OF CANADA FOR THE REGIONS OF QUEBEC
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence de développement économique du Canada pour les régions du Québec

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: FEDERAL ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT AGENCY FOR NORTHERN ONTARIO
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence fédérale de développement économique pour le Nord de l’Ontario

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: FEDERAL ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT AGENCY FOR SOUTHERN ONTARIO
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence fédérale de développement économique pour le Sud de l’Ontario

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: FINANCIAL TRANSACTIONS AND REPORTS ANALYSIS CENTRE OF CANADA
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Centre d’analyse des opérations et déclarations financières du Canada

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Chambre des communes

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures, including payments in respect of the cost of operating Members’ constituency offices
– Contributions
– Authority to expend revenues that it receives during the fiscal year arising from its activities

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Commission de l’immigration et du statut de réfugié

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence canadienne d’évaluation d’impact

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the conduct of assessments by a review panel; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Centre de recherches pour le développement international

Provision.Text: – Payments to the Centre

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Commission mixte internationale (section canadienne)

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures
– Expenses of the Canadian Section, including salaries
– Expenses of studies, surveys and investigations by the Commission under International References
– Expenses of the Commission under the Canada-United States Great Lakes Water Quality Agreement

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Investir au Canada

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Commission du droit du Canada

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Commission des débats des chefs

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bibliothèque et Archives du Canada

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures
– Contributions

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset related expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the provision of access to the collection and from the reproduction of materials from the collection; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act
– Capital expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bibliothèque du Parlement

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures
– Authority to expend revenues that it receives in the fiscal year arising from its activities

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Marine Atlantique S.C.C.

Provision.Text: – Payments to the corporation in respect of the costs of its management
– Payments to the corporation for capital expenditures

– Payments to the corporation for transportation services, including the water transportation services between Nova Scotia and Newfoundland and Labrador and related vessels, terminals and infrastructure

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Comité externe d’examen des griefs militaires

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Commission d’examen des plaintes concernant la police militaire

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Société du Centre national des Arts

Provision.Text: – Payments to the Corporation for operating expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Commission de la capitale nationale

Provision.Text: – Payments to the Commission for operating expenditures
– Payments to the Commission for capital expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Office national du film

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Musée des beaux-arts du Canada

Provision.Text: – Payments to the Gallery for operating and capital expenditures
– Payments to the Gallery for the acquisition of objects for the collection and related costs

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: NATIONAL MUSEUM OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Musée national des sciences et de la technologie

Provision.Text: – Payments to the Museum for operating and capital expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Conseil national de recherches du Canada

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures
– Capital expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions, including the provision of goods and services for the Thirty Meter Telescope

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Secrétariat de l’Office de surveillance des activités en matière de sécurité nationale et de renseignement

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Conseil de recherches en sciences naturelles et en génie

– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Administration du pipe-line du Nord

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bureau du vérificateur général

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset related expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the provision of audit professional services to members of the Canadian Council of Legislative Auditors; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: the inquiries conducted under section 11 of the Auditor General Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: OFFICE OF THE CHIEF ELECTORAL OFFICER
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bureau du directeur général des élections

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: OFFICE OF THE COMMISSIONER FOR FEDERAL JUDICIAL AFFAIRS
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bureau du commissaire à la magistrature fédérale

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures of the Office of the Commissioner for Federal Judicial Affairs

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from the provision of administrative services and judicial training services

– Remuneration, allowances and expenses for judges, including deputy judges of the Supreme Court of Yukon, the Supreme Court of the Northwest Territories and the Nunavut Court of Justice, not provided for by the Judges Act
– Operating expenditures of the Canadian Judicial Council

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: OFFICE OF THE COMMISSIONER OF LOBBYING
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Commissariat au lobbying

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: OFFICE OF THE COMMISSIONER OF OFFICIAL LANGUAGES
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Commissariat aux langues officielles

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bureau du commissaire aux conflits d’intérêts et à l’éthique

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: OFFICE OF THE CORRECTIONAL INVESTIGATOR OF CANADA
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bureau de l’enquêteur correctionnel du Canada

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: OFFICE OF THE DIRECTOR OF PUBLIC PROSECUTIONS
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bureau du directeur des poursuites pénales

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the provision of prosecution and prosecution-related services;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: the provision to Crown corporations, non-federal organizations and international organizations of optional prosecution and prosecution-related services that are consistent with the Office’s mandate; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: OFFICE OF THE GOVERNOR GENERAL’S SECRETARY
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bureau du secrétaire du gouverneur général

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

– Expenditures incurred for former Governors General, including those incurred for their spouses, during their lifetimes and for a period of six months following their deaths, in respect of the performance of activities which devolve on them as a result of their having occupied the office of Governor General

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bureau du commissaire au renseignement

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bureau du directeur parlementaire du budget

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bureau du surintendant des institutions financières

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Commissariats à l’information et à la protection de la vie privée du Canada

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures — Office of the Information Commissioner
– Program expenditures — Office of the Privacy Commissioner
– Contributions — Office of the Privacy Commissioner

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset related expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: PACIFIC ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT AGENCY OF CANADA
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence de développement économique du Pacifique Canada

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Parc Downsview Park Inc.

Provision.Text: – To increase, for the purposes of paragraph 101(1)(b) of the Financial Administration Act, from $100,000,000 to $300,000,000 the amount of money that the corporation is authorized to borrow

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence Parcs Canada

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions, including

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: expenditures on other than federal property; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: payments to provinces and municipalities as contributions towards the cost of undertakings carried out by those bodies
– Capital expenditures
– Amounts credited to the New Parks and Historic Sites Account for the purposes specified in subsection 21(3) of the Parks Canada Agency Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Service de protection parlementaire

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures
– Authority to expend revenues that it receives during the fiscal year arising from its activities

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Commission des libérations conditionnelles du Canada

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from the provision of services under section 29.2 of that Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Conseil d’examen du prix des médicaments brevetés

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bureau du Conseil privé

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures, including

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: operating expenditures of Commissions of Inquiry not otherwise provided for;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: contributions in respect of costs incurred by persons appearing before Commissions of Inquiry; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: the operation of the Prime Minister’s residence

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset related expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the provision of intelligence analysis training; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence de la santé publique du Canada

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from the sale of products, the provision of inspection services and the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act
– Capital expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Commission de la fonction publique

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from the provision of staffing, assessment and counselling services and products and the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: REGISTRAR OF THE SUPREME COURT OF CANADA
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Registraire de la Cour suprême du Canada

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Gendarmerie royale du Canada

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset related expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year including from the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act
– Capital expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year; however, the amount listed for any grant may be increased or decreased, subject to the approval of the Treasury Board
– Contributions
– Payments made

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: in respect of disability and health benefit programs or other arrangements for members of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police whether serving or retired; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: in respect of the administration of those programs or arrangements, including premiums, contributions, benefit payments, fees and other expenditures in respect of those members and for any other persons that the Treasury Board determines

– Authority under paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset payments made in that fiscal year under this vote — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from the provision of contract policing

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: ROYAL CANADIAN MOUNTED POLICE EXTERNAL REVIEW COMMITTEE
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Comité externe d’examen de la Gendarmerie royale du Canada

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: SECRETARIAT OF THE NATIONAL SECURITY AND INTELLIGENCE COMMITTEE OF PARLIAMENTARIANS
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Secrétariat du Comité des parlementaires sur la sécurité nationale et le renseignement

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Sénat

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures, including payments in respect of the cost of operating Senators’ offices and an allowance in lieu of residence to the Speaker of the Senate
– Contributions
– Authority to expend revenues that it receives during the fiscal year arising from its activities

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Services partagés Canada

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year from the provision of information technology services under the Shared Services Canada Act — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from the provision of those services
– Capital expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset capital expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from the provision of information technology services under the Shared Services Canada Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: SOCIAL SCIENCES AND HUMANITIES RESEARCH COUNCIL
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Conseil de recherches en sciences humaines

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Conseil canadien des normes

Provision.Text: – Payments to the Council that are referred to in paragraph 5(a) of the Standards Council of Canada Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Statistique Canada

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset related expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year including from the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Téléfilm Canada

Provision.Text: – Payments to the corporation to be used for the purposes set out in the Telefilm Canada Act

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: THE JACQUES-CARTIER AND CHAMPLAIN BRIDGES INC.
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Les Ponts Jacques-Cartier et Champlain Inc.

Provision.Text: – Payments to the corporation to be applied in payment of the excess of its expenditures over its revenues (exclusive of depreciation on capital structures and reserves) in the operation of the Jacques-Cartier Bridge, the Québec Bridge, the Estacade, the Melocheville Tunnel, the federal sections of the Honoré Mercier Bridge and of the Bonaventure Expressway and in respect of the shoreline redevelopment for the Héritage Champlain project
– Payments to the corporation for the provision of support services to the Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities for projects in Quebec

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Commission des champs de bataille nationaux

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Secrétariat du Conseil du Trésor

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from the provision of internal support services under section 29.2 of that Act and from its other activities

– The payment to each member of the King’s Privy Council for Canada who is a minister without portfolio, or a minister of State who does not preside over a ministry of State, of a salary — paid annually or pro rata for any period less than a year — that does not exceed the salary paid under the Salaries Act, rounded down to the nearest hundred dollars under section 67 of the Parliament of Canada Act, to ministers of State who preside over ministries of State
Government Contingencies
– Authority granted to the Treasury Board to supplement any other appropriation

– Authority granted to the Treasury Board to provide for miscellaneous, urgent or unforeseen expenditures not otherwise provided for — including for the provision of new grants and contributions or for increases to the amounts of grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year — as long as those expenditures are within the legal mandate of the departments or other organizations for which they are made
– Authority to reuse any sums allotted and repaid to this appropriation from other appropriations
Government-wide Initiatives

– Authority granted to the Treasury Board to supplement any other appropriation for the fiscal year in support of the implementation of strategic management initiatives in the federal public administration
Public Service Insurance

– Payments, in respect of insurance, pension or benefit programs or other arrangements, or in respect of the administration of such programs or arrangements, including premiums, contributions, benefits, fees and other expenditures made in respect of the federal public administration, or any part of it, and in respect of any other persons that the Treasury Board determines
– Authority to expend any revenues or other amounts that it receives in respect of insurance, pension or benefit programs or other arrangements

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: to offset premiums, contributions, benefits, fees and other expenditures in respect of those programs or arrangements; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: to provide for the return to eligible employees of the premium refund under subsection 96(3) of the Employment Insurance Act
Operating Budget Carry Forward
– Authority granted to the Treasury Board to supplement any other appropriation for the fiscal year by reason of the operating budget carry forward from the previous fiscal year
Paylist Requirements
– Authority granted to the Treasury Board to supplement any other appropriation for the fiscal year for

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: requirements related to parental and maternity allowances;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: entitlements on cessation of service or employment; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: adjustments that have not been provided from Vote 15, Compensation Adjustments, made to terms and conditions of service or employment of the federal public administration, including the Royal Canadian Mounted Police, as well as of members of the Canadian Forces
Capital Budget Carry Forward
– Authority granted to the Treasury Board to supplement any other appropriation for the fiscal year by reason of the capital budget carry forward from the previous fiscal year

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Tribunal des anciens combattants (révision et appel)

Provision.Text: – Program expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: VIA HFR - VIA TGF Inc.

Provision.Text: – Payments to the corporation for operating and capital expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: VIA Rail Canada Inc.

Provision.Text: – Payments to the corporation in respect of the costs of its management
– Payments to the corporation for capital expenditures

– Payments to the corporation for the provision of rail passenger services in Canada in accordance with contracts entered into under subparagraph (c)(i) of Transport Vote 52d, Appropriation Act No. 1, 1977

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Autorité du pont Windsor-Détroit

Provision.Text: – Payments to the Authority for the discharge of its mandate consistent with its Letters Patent and the Canada-Michigan Crossing Agreement
Based on the Main Estimates 2025–26, the amount granted is $5,542,407,421, which is the total of the amounts of the items set out in column 5 of this Schedule.

Sums granted to His Majesty by this Act for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026 that may be charged to that fiscal year and the following fiscal year ending March 31, 2027 and the purposes for which they are granted.

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence des services frontaliers du Canada

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures

– Authority, as referred to in paragraph 29.1(2)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, to expend in the fiscal year — in order to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year — revenues that it receives in that fiscal year from

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: fees, related to border operations, for the provision of a service or the use of a facility or for a product, right or privilege; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: payments received under contracts entered into by the Agency
– Capital expenditures

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence du revenu du Canada

Provision.Text: – Operating expenditures
– The grants listed in any of the Estimates for the fiscal year
– Contributions
– Authority to make recoverable expenditures in relation to the application of the Canada Pension Plan and the Employment Insurance Act
– Capital expenditures
– Authority to make recoverable expenditures in relation to the application of the Canada Pension Plan and the Employment Insurance Act
